summary: The six-clause canonical JSON Spec is consistent with the Architecture decision, Story acceptance criteria, target-model run-session ownership, Design SSOT parent/child routing, and current implementation boundaries. Every Architecture section and code anchor referenced by the Spec exists; the CLI remains an outer adapter with no reverse import from run-session sources; and merged connector/review orchestration owners are composed rather than duplicated.
inspection_summary: Inspected all six Spec clauses and traced each Story reference, Architecture section, code anchor, inherited behavior, and test surface. Confirmed the closed action profile, current-HEAD pr_ready invariant, explicit human-authority exclusions, typed-stop/decision lifecycle, managed-worktree implementation and separate read-only review, and staged post-PR closure contract. Cross-checked target architecture conformance and the exact-head behavioral acceptance mapping.

clause_results.reason: The default guarded profile, ordered provider fallback, explicit legacy compatibility, and public CLI composition match Architecture Decision and the referenced guarded-run-session, safe-action, and CLI anchors.

The Architecture and implementation agree that only final_prepare can return pr_ready and only for a pr-prepare result bound to the authoritative current managed-worktree HEAD with ready_for_pr_create=true.

The autonomous registry and bounded runtime objectives exclude PR creation, merge, critical waiver, deployment, publication, and material external side effects; Human Decision creation remains guarded-run-session authority.

The typed-stop, resume, cancellation, human-decision, verification failure, repair convergence, and current-HEAD rebind contract is represented consistently in Architecture and the referenced run-session/action/checkpoint modules.

The production connector remains the managed-worktree implementation adapter and Independent Review Orchestration remains the separate read-only lifecycle owner. The referenced repair-HEAD regression exists as IRO-S-7 with equivalent wording and semantics.

The pre-PR acceptance/post-PR delivery split matches Architecture and Story: PR #372, #377, and #382 are predecessor evidence, the final Story and roadmap stay active pre-PR, and CI import, explicit execute merge, and canonical audit remain later explicit lifecycle owners.

reference_validation.note: The S-OCR-5 test_ref case label 'repaired HEAD invalidates the prior review operation checkpoint' is a semantic label rather than an exact test-title match; it resolves to the present test 'IRO-S-7 repair HEAD invalidates the old review checkpoint and dispatches a fresh review'.

judgment_delta.initial_concern: The six-clause Spec could describe a second orchestration layer distinct from the Architecture or current source topology.
judgment_delta.final_judgment: Each clause maps to an existing Architecture section and concrete owner anchor. The only new focused policy owner is inside run-session and is composed by guarded-run-session; no parallel CLI-owned workflow is specified or implemented.

judgment_delta.initial_concern: Spec references to connector and independent review could authorize reimplementation of predecessor Stories.
judgment_delta.final_judgment: S-OCR-5 and S-OCR-6 explicitly classify these contracts as inherited. Current code uses createProductionRuntimeConnectors and createGuardedIndependentReviewRunner from the merged PR #377/#382 modules, preserving their capability, sandbox, identity, lifecycle, provenance, and verdict responsibilities.

judgment_delta.initial_concern: The CLI code_ref in C-OCR-1 could imply a forbidden dependency from the run-session domain back to src/cli.js.
judgment_delta.final_judgment: The reference identifies the public adapter surface only. Reverse-import inspection over all target-model run-session sources returns no src/cli.js dependency, and exact-head conformance independently reports no new reverse dependency.

judgment_delta.initial_concern: The S-OCR-5 repair-checkpoint test_ref label does not exactly equal the current Node test title.
judgment_delta.final_judgment: The referenced file contains the uniquely corresponding IRO-S-7 regression, 'repair HEAD invalidates the old review checkpoint and dispatches a fresh review'. The semantic contract, owner, and executable target are unambiguous, so this wording variation does not create a consistency defect.

judgment_delta.initial_concern: The staged closure clause could conflate pre-PR acceptance with future CI and merge evidence.
judgment_delta.final_judgment: Architecture, Story, Spec, and Design SSOT agree on a two-stage protocol: pre-PR proves predecessor evidence and leaves Story/roadmap active; PR creation, CI import, closure commit, evidence rebind, rereview, re-import, and explicit merge are post-PR lifecycle operations.
